records.summary: I18n TranslationBinding lifecycle rows and target semantics are modeled after runtime rendering joins key and parameter bindings on the same target element.
records.rationale: Aurelia i18n does not treat each t/t.bind/t-params.bind attribute as an isolated binding lifetime. The renderer retrieves or creates one TranslationBinding per target and t-params.bind calls useParameter(...), while I18nKeyEvaluationResult parses semicolon-delimited key expressions such as [title]key;key into runtime target attributes. Semantic-runtime must group rendered TranslationBinding products by target and parse static key-expression targets before projecting positive api.I18nTranslationBindings rows or publishing RuntimeBindingIssue products for AUR4000/AUR4001/AUR4002.
guidance: Inspect framework i18n TranslationBinding and translation renderers before changing this lane; the join point is TranslationBinding.useParameter(...).

Keep translation-key catalog products separate from translation-binding lifecycle diagnostics: catalog completion comes from configuration resources, while lifecycle errors come from rendered binding products.

Treat framework CustomExpression pressure in this lane as translation binding/key-expression pressure first: TranslationBindingCommand uses CustomExpression as an opaque carrier for static translation attribute raw values, and TranslationBinding.create reparses the value as interpolation.

Use api.I18nTranslationBindings and i18n-translation-binding expected effects when a recipe needs to prove successful rendered t/t.bind/t-params.bind lifecycles; rows expose normalized targetProperties/targetKinds because the compiler target for t itself is not the same as TranslationBinding's runtime write target.
Surface i18n lifecycle diagnostics through shared template/app diagnostic projection instead of adding i18n-specific diagnostic rows; the translation-key catalog has its own query/effect lane.

anchors.summary: Static mirror of framework i18n key-expression attribute parsing and target defaulting.
Shared positive/negative target-element grouping primitive for rendered i18n TranslationBinding products.
Public row projection for rendered TranslationBinding target groups.
Exact framework error-code links for AUR4000, AUR4001, and AUR4002.
Synthetic pressure fixture for i18n translation binding diagnostics.
Closed-loop smoke for positive localized catalog and rendered translation-binding verification.
Ground i18n error-code coverage before adding more i18n diagnostics.

records.summary: Static i18n translation resources now have a public query and authoring expected-effect surface.
records.rationale: Generated localized fixtures need to prove that plugin registration admitted translation resources without comparing generated source text. The static i18n catalog materializer already owns I18nTranslationKey products, so api.I18nTranslationKeys and i18n-translation-key expected effects are the correct verification boundary.
guidance: Use api.I18nTranslationKeys when a caller needs static translation catalog rows for completion, fixture verification, or future hover/coverage work.
Use i18n-translation-key expected effects when a recipe needs to prove static I18nConfiguration init resources reopened as semantic facts.

Keep dynamic backend loaders, runtime language switching, missing-key lifecycle diagnostics, and translation catalog authoring as separate frontiers; this lane only proves static catalog availability.
Pressure summaries report i18n key counts without printing translation keys, so external app scans do not promote app-specific catalog names into tracked output.

anchors.summary: Public row projection for static translation key products.
Expected-effect observation now handles i18n-translation-key rows.
Generated localized form recipe that uses static I18nConfiguration resources.
Closed-loop smoke for localized generated fixture verification.
